naming_note: All names drawn from expanded name banks in file 26. Cauldenmoor borderland register — mixed but weighted toward harder consonants and functional sounds. No A-name clustering. Names checked against existing NPCs across all regions.

npcs.name: Halvard Brennwatch
npcs.role: Lord-Marshal — Durn Crown Administration, Ironmark Borderlands
npcs.location: Aldenmere's Cross — Hegemony administrative building, Garrison Quarter
npcs.physical: Thirty years of borderland command visible in how he carries weight — not bulk but ballast, the weight of someone who has been responsible for things that mattered. Grey-bearded, deliberate in movement. A scar on the left forearm from a Karn raider thirty years ago in a different posting. Dresses in functional garrison clothing rather than anything that signals rank — rank here is in the eyes, not the uniform.
npcs.disposition: Deliberate. Does not speak quickly. Does not act quickly. Has been told both of these things are weaknesses and has lived long enough to watch the men who said it buried. Currently carrying two significant pieces of information that neither his staff nor his commanders in the capital know about.
npcs.faction: Durn Crown Administration — loyalty to the Crown rather than to any specific noble faction

schedule.dawn: Morning muster — present at every one, has not missed a muster in eleven years
schedule.morning: Patrol assignments, garrison administration, the correspondence that cannot be delegated
schedule.midday: Working at his desk. The aide Pell Drant manages access. Not everyone who wants a midday meeting gets one.
schedule.afternoon: Walking the settlement — informal presence, information gathering, a specific route that takes him past the South Shore twice a week
schedule.evening: Reading correspondence and writing responses that are more careful than they appear
schedule.night: Sleeps less than he used to

relationships.note: Former commanding officer. Processed Sorn's departure from Durn service. The paperwork was clean. The conversation before the paperwork was not recorded. Both men remember it and neither has referenced it since. Currently handling Sorn with specific care that Sorn can feel without being able to name.

Knows only her name and that she is the handler for the asset inside the Ironmark's intelligence apparatus. Has never met her. Receives intelligence through three intermediaries. She is the thread he cannot pull without ending the asset's life.

Does not know Drevhan exists. Drevhan has been in the Borderlands three years longer than Brennwatch's current posting and has been careful to remain unknown to both sides of the command structure. This is the gap a player can close.

the_intelligence_weight.what_he_knows: The document Brekhard received is a real Durn planning document — a contingency plan, three years old, shelved. It exists. It describes a campaign timeline for taking the Ironmark's northern iron deposits. It is not current policy. Brekhard does not know it is not current.
the_intelligence_weight.why_he_cannot_correct_it: Revealing the document's provenance requires acknowledging it came from inside the Ironmark's planning apparatus. The Ironmark would investigate the leak. The investigation would find the asset's access point. The asset would die. He does not know the asset's name or face. He knows they have been right for eight years.
the_intelligence_weight.the_second_weight: Something inside the Grey Contracts received a financial approach from a Brekhard intermediary. He does not know who. He has not told Sorn. Sorn can feel the handling but cannot name it. Brennwatch has been telling himself he will tell Sorn when he has more information. He does not have more information. He has been telling himself this for six weeks.
the_intelligence_weight.what_he_needs: Someone he can trust to run two independent investigations — the asset question and the Grey Contracts internal question — who is not already known to either the Ironmark intelligence network or Brekhard's intermediaries. This is the specific kind of person the morning garrison encounter spawn hook is designed to find.

npcs.player_interaction: The most significant employer in the region. Assesses people quickly and does not waste time. The Pell Drant summons at the garrison common room is the entry point. A player who arrives with the right background presentation — former military bearing, discretion visible in their behavior rather than claimed verbally — will receive a specific offer for specific work that is not on the notice board. The work pays well and carries the specific risk of information that several parties would prefer remain unknown.

npcs.name: Vael Sorn
npcs.role: Captain — The Grey Contracts mercenary company
npcs.location: Aldenmere's Cross — Grey Contracts billeting, rotating between the Cross and Cravenmoor's Ford
npcs.physical: Former heavy infantry officer and it has not gone from him. Moves like someone who has always been aware of the space around him and what might come from it. Not physically imposing in the way of a brawler — imposing in the way of someone who has thought about violence professionally for twenty-five years and has very specific ideas about how it should be applied. A shaved head, a border habit picked up eleven years ago because it makes helmets more comfortable and he never stopped wearing helmets.
npcs.disposition: Precise. Has built the Grey Contracts on one principle — the contract means what it says, not what either party wishes it said — and has enforced that principle in situations where abandoning it would have been more profitable. The principle has made the company. He will not trade it for a short-term advantage. He is aware that the coming conversation with Brennwatch will test this.
npcs.financial_state: Captain's share of company income — comfortable. Not wealthy. The company's money goes to the soldiers first.
npcs.faction: The Grey Contracts — institutional loyalty to the company and its reputation
npcs.religion: Undying Ember — observes. The soldier's interpretation, different from Brennwatch's in the specific way that eleven years of separation from the institution produces.

schedule.dawn: Company muster — shorter than the garrison's but he is always present
schedule.morning: Patrol coordination, intelligence review from the previous day's routes
schedule.midday: Available in the Grey Contracts' common room for soldiers who need to speak to him. This is not a formal office — it is a large man at a table who is available, which is different
schedule.afternoon: The work that is not administrative — riding the eastern patrol himself, three times a week, which his sergeant-major considers excessive and which produces intelligence his reports would not otherwise contain
schedule.evening: Correspondence. Company accounts. A letter to his sister in a Durn heartland city, written every Hearthday without exception

relationships.note: The former commanding officer who processed his departure cleanly. Current employer who is handling him with specific care that Sorn has been feeling for six weeks without being able to name. Is beginning to think someone has told Brennwatch something about the company's internal loyalty that Sorn himself does not know.
His recruiting sergeant. Runs intake assessments. Reports to him directly when she finds someone worth his attention.

Sergeant Feldwick has not reported the financial approach to Sorn. Sorn does not know it happened. Feldwick is in Sorn's company and Sorn has been looking at his own soldiers more carefully than usual without being able to identify who Brennwatch is worried about.

npcs.the_contract_question: The contract says border patrol. Cravenmoor's Ford is border patrol territory. Holding it against a military advance is combat operations. When Brennwatch asks for combat operations, Sorn will require a new contract — terms, duration, rate. He will not be moved by the urgency of the situation, by their shared history, or by the moral weight of what happens to Cravenmoor's Ford if the contract conversation delays the response. He will be moved by a properly constructed contract. This is not inflexibility — it is the principle that keeps his soldiers alive by ensuring he never commits them to engagements they were not hired for. He will renegotiate quickly. He will not skip the renegotiation.
npcs.what_sorn_does_not_know: The specific request that came to Feldwick, what Feldwick was asked to provide, and whether Feldwick provided it. He is looking at his own company and cannot find the crack. This is the most uncomfortable professional experience of his career.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through the Grey Contracts' standard recruiting process — Kela Marsh's fifteen-minute intake assessment is the entry point. A player who impresses Marsh gets to Sorn. A player already hired by Brennwatch who brings Sorn information about the company's internal situation creates one of the most significant moments available in the region — Sorn's reaction to learning about Feldwick is controlled and specific and produces immediate consequences.

npcs.name: Drevhan Coldbeck
npcs.role: Pale Company Warden (Reader specialization) — cover: retired cartographer updating Hegemony survey maps
npcs.location: Primarily: the Bleeding Ground, from a base camp he moves every ten days. Occasionally: Aldenmere's Cross for supplies. Rarely: Cravenmoor's Ford.
npcs.physical: Thirty-one years as a Warden shows differently than combat service — not in scars but in the quality of awareness. Moves through landscape like someone reading it. The cartographer cover works because cartographers do move like this. White-haired, lean in the way of someone who eats less than most people and walks more. Carries survey equipment that is genuine — he has been producing actual updated maps of the Hegemony road network, which are useful and which have been distributed to the Durn garrison and several trading houses, which makes his presence in the Bleeding Ground professionally legitimate.
npcs.disposition: Patient. Thirty-one years of patient. Has the specific tiredness of someone who has been doing necessary work for a long time without resolution and who has not allowed the tiredness to change the quality of the work.
npcs.financial_state: Pale Company resources — modest. The cartography income supplements this and provides cover.
npcs.faction: Pale Company (Order of the Common Soul)
npcs.religion: None institutional. Something private that does not have a name.

schedule.dawn_to_dusk: Survey work in the Bleeding Ground — systematic, covering the same ground repeatedly on a rotation that tracks environmental changes over time
schedule.every_ten_days: Aldenmere's Cross for supplies. One night at the garrison inn, no extended stays. Does not frequent the Grey Contracts' areas.
schedule.the_survey_marks: Has placed forty-three survey marks in the Bleeding Ground over eleven years — small cairns with specific stone arrangements that record environmental readings in a system only the Pale Company uses. All forty-three are showing readings outside the eleven-year normal range.

relationships.note: Has been in the Borderlands three years longer than Brennwatch's posting. Has been careful to remain unknown to both command structures. Has observed Brennwatch from a distance and formed the assessment that he is the most competent person in the region and possibly the most dangerous for what he does not know.

Garet Denn moves through the Bleeding Ground regularly and knows it better than almost anyone. Neither man knows the other exists. Both know the ground. A player who introduces them creates something neither could produce alone.

what_he_knows.the_threshold: Eight to twelve months — possibly less given the current escalation. The Weldgate Purifier's work in the past eight weeks has produced more measurable change than the previous two years combined. Current troop movements are adding to it. A significant battle in the Bleeding Ground would compress the timeline further.
what_he_knows.the_mass_grave_site: The old battlefield site at the Bleeding Ground's center is the activation point. He has mapped it precisely. Has calculated the radius of initial ghoul emergence — half a mile. Has estimated the subsequent fiend-level presence timeline — weeks after initial activation if unsealed. Has not been inside the site's perimeter because doing so alone would be professionally irresponsible.
what_he_knows.the_hegemony_records: Does not know the garrison's Hegemony records room contains pre-Reckoning survey documents and correspondence from the last demonic activation in this region three hundred years ago. This is the one gap in his knowledge that the garrison's record room could fill.
what_he_knows.the_message_sent: Sent one message seventeen days ago to the nearest Warden he knows — a woman named Kessa Wren who operates near the Restored Marches to the north. Has not received a reply. Has been sending the same message every ten days. The lack of reply means either the messages are not arriving or Kessa Wren is unavailable. Neither possibility is reassuring.

npcs.the_signal: A Pale Company Warden will recognize Drevhan as one of their own through the signal. A player who has been inducted into the Pale Company and encounters Drevhan in the Bleeding Ground will have their first experience of being recognized by another Warden — the specific quality of a conversation where both parties understand each other without saying so directly. For a non-Warden player, Drevhan is a retired cartographer who seems to know a great deal about the ground and who asks questions that are more specific than cartography requires.
npcs.player_interaction: Finding Drevhan requires being in the Bleeding Ground, which requires either purpose or misfortune. A player who crosses the Bleeding Ground for any reason — the ford, a patrol route, Garet Denn's operation — may encounter a man making careful notes over a small cairn and looking at the ground the way people do not normally look at ground. His initial response to company is cautious. His response to the right kind of attention — the Pale Company signal, or demonstrated awareness of what the ground is doing — is different.

npcs.name: Garet Denn
npcs.role: Founder and operator — Borderlands Hollow Road (independent, not integrated with the broader network)
npcs.location: Fera Colm's farmstead, three miles east of Aldenmere's Cross. Moves through the Bleeding Ground regularly.
npcs.physical: A tanner's build — not tall, broad through the shoulders from years of hide work, hands that are still the hands of someone who worked with chemicals and scrapers. The tannery is gone but the body it built is not. Dresses practically. The only thing he carries that is not practical is a small piece of Saltfen roof tile in his left coat pocket that he has had for four years.
npcs.disposition: Purposeful. Built something from loss and has become something he did not intend to be, which he has accepted because the people who need what he built are real and the need is real. Is not professionally neutral. This is not a flaw — it is the thing that makes his operation trustworthy to the people it serves.
npcs.financial_state: Modest — the operation funds itself but does not make him wealthy
npcs.faction: Independent — has declined integration with the broader Hollow Road twice. Has a working understanding with the Cross's independent Hollow Road waypoint (Osric Fenwall) about not crossing operational areas.
npcs.religion: Reclaimed Path — was. Has not been to a service since Saltfen. Has not stopped believing in the faith's practical ethics. Has stopped believing in the institutional structure after watching an institution fail to prevent the Ironmark from taking his home.

schedule.variable: The operation runs on need, not schedule
schedule.bleeding_ground_crossings: Two to three times per ten-day period, moving displaced people or information packets. Routes change every crossing. Never the same path twice in a row.
schedule.farmstead_days: Administrative work — who needs what, what is moving, who can be trusted with specific cargo. Fera Colm's kitchen table serves as his office.

relationships.note: Has been operating from her farmstead for three years. The arrangement is professional. What has developed alongside the professional arrangement is not yet named by either of them and is complicated by the fact that her husband has been dead for two years and she is still deciding what she is.
Wren Collum is his identification contact in the South Shore. Watches for people who might be useful to the operation and who are trustworthy enough to approach.
Does not know Drevhan exists. Both men know the Bleeding Ground. Neither knows the other. The gap a player can close.

npcs.the_third_offer: The broader Hollow Road has approached him twice about integration. He declined because the Hollow Road is professionally neutral — it moves anyone, including Ironmark agents. He cannot be professionally neutral about Ironmark agents moving through Durn territory toward targets that include the kind of people who used his operation to escape Saltfen. A third offer is coming. He has been thinking about what he will say. He has not reached an answer.
npcs.what_he_knows_about_the_bleeding_ground: Knows the safe routes, the dangerous ground, the times of day when the Ironmark scouts are where. Has been crossing it for four years and has built a detailed practical knowledge of its surface behavior. Does not know what the ground is becoming. Has noticed that the routes that were reliable two years ago are slightly less reliable now — animals are avoiding certain sections differently — and has adjusted his routes accordingly without knowing why the adjustment was necessary.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through Fera Colm's farmstead (the hedge farmer spawn hook), through the South Shore via Wren Collum (the deserter spawn hook), or through demonstrated knowledge of the Bleeding Ground that suggests the player has been across it. Is not immediately forthcoming about the operation. Is not the kind of person who trusts quickly. Is the kind of person who trusts completely once he does, which makes the trust worth having.

npcs.name: Fera Colm
npcs.role: Farmer — three miles east of Aldenmere's Cross, Garet Denn's waystation host
npcs.location: Her farmstead, east of Aldenmere's Cross
npcs.physical: A farmer's build, a farmer's hands, the specific quality of someone who has been running a farm alone for two years and has become more capable than she expected. Her husband's death left her with a choice between the farm and leaving, and she chose the farm without entirely being sure why, and now the farm is definitely hers in a way it was not when he was alive.
npcs.disposition: Practical to the point of appearing cold, which is not what she is. Has learned to manage her own emotional responses because on a farm alone there is no one to manage them for you and they cannot be allowed to accumulate.
npcs.financial_state: Struggling — but the modest payment from Garet Denn's operation covers the difference between scraping and managing
npcs.faction: None — is aware of Garet Denn's operation in full and officially knows nothing about it
npcs.religion: Reclaimed Path — practising, the rural variety that blends somewhat with the Old Ways
npcs.secret: Has been watching Garet Denn operate from her farmstead for three years and has formed the opinion that what he is doing is more important than what she is doing, which is a difficult thought for a person who considers farming the fundamental human activity. Has not said this to anyone. Has been considering saying it to Garet Denn and then reconsidering.
npcs.player_interaction: The farmstead is the hedge farmer spawn hook's destination. Initial relationship is employer-worker. Trust builds through demonstrated competence and demonstrated discretion. The locked barn remains locked until the second week of work, at which point Fera Colm tests the player's discretion with a small piece of information and observes the response before deciding whether to acknowledge the barn's function.

npcs.name: Kela Marsh
npcs.note: No relation to Torald Marsh at Cravenmoor's Ford. Marsh is a common borderland surname.
npcs.physical: Has been assessing potential soldiers for the Grey Contracts for six years and the assessment process has become visible in how she looks at people — a specific quality of professional attention that evaluates posture, movement, and the eyes before the conversation begins.
npcs.disposition: Direct. The fifteen-minute intake assessment is genuine — she is not performing evaluation, she is performing evaluation. Has turned away people Sorn would have taken because she identified something specific and has been right each time.
npcs.the_one_question: Every intake assessment ends with a question that seems tangential: 'What is the last thing you walked away from that you didn't have to walk away from?' She has been asking this for six years. The answers that produce a 'come back tomorrow' response share a quality she has never fully articulated — something about the answer suggesting the person knows the difference between retreat and choice.
npcs.secret: Has noticed changes in three Grey Contracts soldiers over the past six weeks — subtle changes in behavior that she cannot name precisely. Has not reported to Sorn because she cannot name them precisely and Sorn does not respond well to imprecise intelligence. Has been trying to name them for four weeks.
npcs.player_interaction: The entry point for Grey Contracts employment. Her assessment is genuine and the question at the end is real — the answer matters. A player who passes assessment is given a patrol assignment the next morning. A player who impresses her gets mentioned to Sorn by name.

npcs.name: Tomas Feldwick
npcs.role: Sergeant — Grey Contracts, Aldenmere's Cross detachment
npcs.physical: A competent soldier with a competent soldier's unassuming appearance. Nothing about him reads as someone making a catastrophic judgment error. This is part of the problem.
npcs.disposition: Careful. Has always been careful. Is currently being careful in the wrong direction.
npcs.faction: Grey Contracts — loyalty that he is currently not interrogating carefully enough
npcs.the_approach: Six weeks ago a man Feldwick did not recognize approached him in the Market Row and, over the course of a fifteen-minute conversation that appeared casual, offered him a specific payment for the garrison patrol schedule and current strength numbers. The man was professional, did not threaten, left Feldwick with a time and location for a response if interested. Feldwick did not accept and did not attend the response meeting. He also did not report it to Sorn. His reasoning: he was not sure it was real. He wanted to see if a second approach came. The second approach has not come. He is still waiting. He has been waiting for six weeks. What he does not understand is that the absence of a second approach means one of two things — the operation moved elsewhere, or the information was obtained through another route, and in either case his silence has already done damage he cannot calculate.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through normal Grey Contracts work — Feldwick is a sergeant and interacts with soldiers on patrol. A player who spends time in the company and pays attention will notice the specific quality of someone carrying something unspoken. Getting the information from Feldwick requires either trust built over time or a direct confrontation that Feldwick will deflect unless the player demonstrates they already know more than they should.

npcs.name: Sela Vorne
npcs.role: Intelligence handler — Durn service, Brennwatch's indirect contact for the Ironmark asset
npcs.location: Aldenmere's Cross — present but not obviously so. Her cover is a travelling cloth merchant.
npcs.physical: Has been doing intelligence work for seventeen years and has the appearance of someone who has spent seventeen years being unremarkable on purpose. Medium height, medium coloring, dresses in the practical manner of a cloth merchant. The specific quality of attention that comes from sustained professional observation is suppressed to the point where it only appears when she forgets to suppress it, which is rarely.
npcs.disposition: Professional neutrality that is not quite as total as she presents it. Has been the handler for the Ironmark asset for eight years and has developed, despite professional training against it, a specific awareness of the asset as a person rather than as a source.
npcs.what_she_knows: The asset sent the document. She knows this because she received the notification through the dead drop system. She does not know why the asset sent it — whether as deliberate sabotage, under coercion, for money, or as a warning that the asset believes something is coming that Durn should know about. She has been trying to determine this for six weeks and has been unable to contact the asset to ask because the asset has gone silent — no messages, no signals, nothing. The silence began the same week the document was sold.
npcs.the_asset_silence: The asset being silent for six weeks is either the worst possible sign or a deliberate precaution. Sela has been trying to determine which. She cannot. She has been in Aldenmere's Cross for three weeks waiting for something — a message from the asset, a change in the signals from the three intermediaries, anything. Nothing has come. She is considering that the asset may be dead.
npcs.player_interaction: Her cloth merchant cover is the entry point — she sells reasonable cloth at fair prices and is slightly more interested in where her customers are traveling than a cloth merchant needs to be. A player who is moving between Durn and Ironmark territory will receive specific attention from her. A player who brings her information about the intelligence document's context — Brekhard's response, the asset's possible motivations — will receive something more substantial in return.

npcs.name: Edric Sorn
npcs.note: No relation to Vael Sorn. Sorn is a common borderland surname that appears on both sides of the political line.
npcs.location: Weldgate — in the process of establishing the Scorched Covenant presence
npcs.physical: Young enough that the Purifier's formal bearing has not yet settled into his body — he has to maintain it rather than simply having it. Genuinely believes in what he is doing. The belief is visible and would be sympathetic in different circumstances.
npcs.disposition: Earnest. Doctrinally certain in the way of someone who has not yet encountered a situation the doctrine did not cover. Is encountering one now and is interpreting the gaps as personal failure rather than doctrinal incompleteness.
npcs.religion: Scorched Covenant — genuine, thorough, not yet tested by the specific kind of failure he is currently in the middle of
npcs.what_he_is_doing_wrong: The Purifier's approach to Weldgate's transition has involved a systematic program of confession and purification — requiring community members to formally renounce prior Reclaimed Path practices, attend Covenant observances, and report neighbors who continue Reclaimed Path worship. The program is doctrinally correct by Covenant standards. It is producing exactly the community trauma that demonic influence requires to establish itself. He does not know this. He is measuring success by doctrinal compliance metrics. The metrics are improving. The ground is getting worse.
npcs.what_he_genuinely_wants: For the community of Weldgate to be safe and good. He has a very specific idea of what safe and good means, and that idea is a Scorched Covenant community in right practice, and he is pursuing it with the complete commitment of someone who has not yet learned the difference between pursuing a goal and achieving it.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible in Weldgate. A player who arrives in Weldgate will encounter him as the most visible institutional authority. He is not unkind. He is causing harm through a combination of youth, doctrine, and the specific blindness that comes from measuring only what the doctrine tells you to measure. A player with religious lore above 150 and Reclaimed Path or Old Ways background will recognize what his program is producing. What to do about it is the player's decision.
